structural_floor_note: The series begins at fiscal year 2002 because that is when the City of Colorado Springs first implemented GASB Statement 34 (the consolidated accrual-basis reporting framework). This is documented explicitly in the 2005 CAFR Table 1 footnote, the 2009 CAFR Table 1 footnote, and the 2005 CAFR auditor's report. Pre-2002 data exists only at the fund level in the old CAFR format and is not comparable to the post-GASB-34 series.
terminology_note: Pre-2012 filings use 'net assets' and 'invested in capital assets, net of related debt'. 2012 onward uses 'net position' and 'net investment in capital assets' due to GASB 63 (effective Jan 1, 2012), which also introduced deferred outflows/inflows of resources. Values in this file are normalized to the modern nomenclature regardless of source-filing vintage.

correction_log.issue: Prior response identified 2010 total primary government net position as $2,338,901,049
correction_log.root_cause: Mis-identified the 'Net investment in capital assets' component row as the 'Total primary government net position' row during text extraction of 2019 CAFR Table 1
correction_log.effect_on_analysis: The purported 2010→2011 +42% jump does not exist; actual growth was +5.7%

correction_log.issue: Prior response identified 2015 total primary government net position as $2,977,767,902
correction_log.root_cause: Same extraction error repeated on a different year's column
correction_log.verification: Confirmed $2,933,293,322 in 2015, 2016, 2018 CAFRs Table 1; reconciles with 2015 CAFR MD&A statement of -$194.4M / -6.2% decline
correction_log.effect_on_analysis: The GASB 68 pension adoption impact in 2015 was -6.2% ($194.4M), not -4.8%. The 2015→2016 recovery was +4.9% ($143.4M), not +3.3%.

series.note: GASB 34 implementation year — first year of consolidated accrual reporting. This is the structural floor of the dataset.
Beginning-to-ending delta (+$54.6M) differs from Statement of Activities change (+$75.9M) because of early GASB 34 classification adjustments.
+$231M jump in Invested-in-capital-assets row reflects GASB 34 infrastructure retroactive capitalization (phase-in for mid-sized governments was through 2005)
Second year of GASB 34 infrastructure phase-in completion. 2005 CAFR documents PPRTA 1% sales tax effective Jan 1, 2005 following Nov 2004 voter approval.
Great Recession — Utilities investment portfolio losses, MHS operational pressure. Unrestricted business-type NP fell from $491.6M to $310.2M.

Memorial Health System lease and transfer to Poudre Valley Health Care (UCHealth affiliate) effective October 1, 2012. Nine months of MHS operational expenses ($431M) still booked. $300M MHS debt defeased at transfer. GASB 60 and 63 adopted Jan 1, 2012.
GASB 65 adoption Jan 1, 2013 — ~$80M prior-period adjustment. Statement-of-Activities change (+$32.4M per MD&A) does not reconcile with beginning-to-ending difference (-$47.8M).
GASB 68 adoption — net pension liability first recognized on balance sheet. Unrestricted NP flipped to -$142.8M. 2014 NOT restated at time of adoption.
GF unrestricted briefly below GFOA 16.7% minimum at 14.7%. 2C Road Tax fund created (0.62% added to city sales tax).
Stormwater fee approved by voters November 2017
GASB 75 OPEB adoption — $113.0M prior-period adjustment. Stormwater fee takes effect.
Stormwater enterprise first full year (+$7.9M to business-type NP)
COVID + Utilities coal-plant decommissioning impairment of $67.3M. Governmental +$72.6M / +5.3%, Business-type -$67.3M / -3.0%.
Post-pandemic rebound + natural gas price shock pass-through to Utilities. Largest single-year gain in series.
GASB 100/101 — compensated absences recognition, $13.67M prior-period adjustment.

cagrs.methodology_note: CAGR computed from ending values; does not adjust for GASB restatements, which affect comparability across GASB 68 (2015) and GASB 75 (2018). Bracketed 'underlying' rates would be ~0.3% higher per restatement absorbed.

gasb_adoption_events.topic: basic financial statements — management's discussion and analysis for state and local governments
gasb_adoption_events.impact: STRUCTURAL FLOOR of this dataset. First year of consolidated accrual-basis reporting. Everything pre-2002 is fund-level only and not comparable.
renamed 'net assets' to 'net position'; restructured balance sheet presentation

gasb_adoption_events.topic: items previously reported as assets and liabilities

gasb_adoption_events.impact: lease liabilities and right-of-use assets booked

sales_tax_rate_history.note: TOPS begins July 1, 1997; city base rate reduced to 2.00% prospectively
Wait — this shows 3.10% in 2005 CAFR. Reconciliation: PSST began Jan 1, 2002 but full-year overlapping total was 3.10% per Table 6. Table 6 rate values are annual average effective rates.
2C Road Tax adds 0.62%: city_direct rises to 3.12%

structural_changes_affecting_comparabili.event: Memorial Health System facilities leased and operations transferred to Poudre Valley Health Care (UCHealth affiliate). Nine months of MHS operations ($431M expenses) still in 2012.
Governmental expense categories reorganized — 'Culture and recreation', 'Urban redevelopment and housing', 'Economic development' consolidated into broader functions
2C Road Repair, Maintenance and Improvement Sales Tax Fund created as new major governmental fund
Stormwater enterprise fund activated following November 2017 voter approval
Stormwater enterprise fund first full year of operations
Utilities board decision to decommission coal-fired power plants — $67.3M capital asset impairment. Sustainable Energy Plan implementation.
Forvis Mazars, LLP replaces FORVIS, LLP as auditor of record due to firm merger
